---
title: Relative rural wage gains outpace nonrural
description: Line chart showing dollar wage changes since the Great Recession for
  rural and nonrural areas.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-dollar-wage-change-since-gr-lc/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Labor Market
  - Economic Indicators
tags:
  - QCEW
  - Wages
  - Rural vs. Nonrural
  - Line Chart
image: X_dollar_wage_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: Relative rural wage gains outpace nonrural
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
  - bls-cpi-deflators
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This chart tracks real dollar wage changes in rural vs nonrural areas since 2007, showing that relative wage gains have been stronger in rural areas.](./X_dollar_wage_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

This chart tracks real dollar wage changes in rural vs nonrural areas since 2007, showing that relative wage gains have been stronger in rural areas.

## Key Findings

- Rural wages have grown faster in relative terms than nonrural wages
- Both rural and nonrural real wages have increased since 2007
- The rural-nonrural wage gap has narrowed slightly

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/wage_change_lc.R` in the producing project.


::: {.callout-note}
## Dangling references

The following slugs are referenced by this project but do not yet have nodes in Dataverse. They are intentionally preserved as future content needs:

- `dataset/bls-cpi-deflators`
:::
